steps.title: Identify corresponding sides in similar triangles
steps.content:
Since △ QPR ~ △ STR, the vertices correspond in order: Q ↔ S, P ↔ T, R ↔ R. From the figure, both triangles have right angles at P and T, with vertical and horizontal legs. The corresponding sides are:
  • QP ↔ ST (vertical legs)
  • PR ↔ TR (horizontal legs)
  • QR ↔ SR (hypotenuses)

steps.title: Set up a proportion using known pairs
steps.content:
For similar triangles, the ratios of corresponding sides are equal. We know QP = 18, ST = 11, and QR = 30. Using the pair of hypotenuses and one known pair:
$$\frac{QP}{ST} = \frac{QR}{SR}$$
Substitute the known values:
$$\frac{18}{11} = \frac{30}{SR}$$

steps.title: Solve for SR using cross multiplication
steps.content:
Cross multiply to isolate SR:
$$18 \cdot SR = 30 \cdot 11$$
$$18 \cdot SR = 330$$
$$SR = \frac{330}{18}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
